task: Build /pulse multi-page experience (Overview/Trending/Compare/Calculator/Guides) on top of existing data + backfill DLD to 24 months.

stages.spec:
Build the multi-page /pulse experience. 5 sub-pages with shared sub-navigation.

WORKING DIR: /Users/zoop/binayah-properties

ROUTING:
- Add a Pulse sub-navigation pill bar component (PulseSubNav.tsx) — sticky below the main Navbar, visible on all /pulse/* routes.
- Sub-nav items: Overview / Trending / Compare / Calculator / Guides — uses Next.js usePathname() to detect active route.

PAGES TO BUILD:

1) /pulse (Overview) — existing route. Just add the sub-nav at top. Keep current Market and Binayah tabs unchanged. Sub-nav highlights "Overview" as active.

2) /pulse/trending — NEW route at src/app/[locale]/pulse/trending/page.tsx + TrendingClient.tsx.
   - "Biggest movers" section: read /api/market-data transactions.monthly[]. If we have N months, compute MoM avgPpsf change; sort by absolute change; show top 5 risers + top 5 fallers.
     Note: with only 4 months of DLD data, MoM is the closest equivalent to "weekly". Label it as "Latest Period Movers" so it is honest.
   - "New launches this month" section: fetch /api/projects?status=Off-Plan and filter to those with launchDate within last 30 days (or createdAt if launchDate missing). Show 6 cards: project name, developer, community, starting price, payment plan if available.
   - "Featured insight" section: grab first article from /api/market-data.news that has imageUrl; render full card linking out.
   - "Weekly summary" section: a 4-stat strip auto-generated from latest monthly entry — count, totalValue, avgPrice, avgPpsf — phrased as a sentence in t() with ICU args.
   - Share buttons row at bottom (WhatsApp / X / LinkedIn / Copy Link) — use real share URLs.

3) /pulse/compare — NEW route at src/app/[locale]/pulse/compare/page.tsx + CompareClient.tsx.
   - Top control: toggle "Communities" | "Developers"
   - Search input below the toggle.
   - Selected items chip row (max 3). Click chip to deselect.
   - When items < 2: show 4 preset buttons (Waterfront Luxury / High Yield / Urban Core / Emerging Stars) — clicking each pre-fills 3 specific community slugs we know exist.
   - When items >= 2: show comparison table:
     * Communities mode: rows = price/sqft, yield, transaction volume (from byArea if community exists in DLD), off-plan share (from market-stats); columns = selected items; highlight winning cell per row.
     * Developers mode: rows = total projects, off-plan projects, avg starting price, communities active in; columns = selected developers.
   - Empty state: "Pick communities to compare" with the preset buttons.

4) /pulse/calculator — NEW route at src/app/[locale]/pulse/calculator/page.tsx + CalculatorClient.tsx.
   - Inputs (left column on desktop, stacked on mobile):
     * Community: dropdown from yieldByArea (only show areas with real yield data)
     * Budget: number input AED (min 100k, max 100M, step 50k)
     * Property type: Apartment / Villa / Townhouse / Studio (button group)
     * Purpose: Rent out / Resell / Live in (button group)
     * Financing: Cash / Mortgage (button group; if Mortgage, show down-payment % slider, default 20%)
   - Outputs (right column / below on mobile), live-updating via useMemo:
     * Estimated property size (sqft) = budget / avgPpsf
     * Annual rental = budget * (yield / 100)
     * Gross yield % = community yield from yieldByArea
     * Net yield = gross * 0.85 (assume 15% costs)
     * 3-year projected value = budget * (1 + growthRate)^3
     * 5-year projected value = same with ^5
     * Comparison strip: 5-year ROI vs AED savings and S&P 500
     * Investment rating badge: Strong (yield >= 7), Moderate (5-7), Weak (<5).
   - Share buttons: WhatsApp prefilled with summary, Email mailto, Copy report link.

5) /pulse/guides — NEW route at src/app/[locale]/pulse/guides/page.tsx + GuidesClient.tsx.
   - 6 authored guides in src/lib/pulse-guides.ts
   - Each card: category pill, read time, views count, title, 2-line description, hover-lift with arrow CTA.

6) /pulse/guides/[slug] — NEW dynamic route.
   - Look up guide in pulse-guides.ts by slug; notFound() if missing.
   - Layout: hero with category + read time + views, h1 title, description, then article body.
   - Bottom: related communities cards, CTA to /contact or /services.

DEMO PATH for QA: visit /pulse, click Trending, click Compare, click Calculator, click Guides, click first guide card.
stages.name: Frontend pages

stages.spec:
Backfill DLD data from current 4 months to 24 months.

WORKING DIR: /Users/zoop/binayah-api

TASKS:
1. In src/services/fetchers/dld-fetcher.ts -- add the export keyword to importDateRange:
   Change: async function importDateRange(
   To:     export async function importDateRange(
   (The function already exists at line 153; this is a one-word change.)

2. Create scripts/dld-backfill.ts:
   - Imports importDateRange from ../src/services/fetchers/dld-fetcher (now exported after task 1)
   - Imports DldTransaction model and mongoose connect/disconnect
   - Loops month by month from 24 months ago to current month
   - Skips months that already have records (count check via DldTransaction.countDocuments({ transactionDate: { $gte, $lt } }))
   - Logs progress per month
   - Closes mongo connection cleanly at end
   - Add a brief comment at the top noting: to run on Render, use the Render shell or a one-off job: npx tsx scripts/dld-backfill.ts

3. Add npm script in package.json:
   "dld-backfill": "tsx scripts/dld-backfill.ts"
   (tsx ^4.21.0 is already in dependencies -- no new deps needed)

4. Run a test build: npm run build to ensure tsc compiles cleanly.

DEMO PATH for QA: code review only -- actual data backfill is a manual run after this commit lands.

findings.issue: Skip threshold of 100 records can cause silent partial-month gaps. A month where only the first page (100 records) was imported on a prior partial run will be marked done even if the month has thousands of transactions. Not a blocker for a one-shot backfill script where re-runs are expected to be supervised, but worth knowing.
findings.suggestedFix: Document in script comment that skip threshold assumes a month has at most 100 real transactions OR that a month with fewer than 100 total DLD records is acceptable to re-skip. Alternatively raise threshold or skip only if count equals the previously-stored total.

reviewerReport.summary: 6/7 points clean. One minor skip-threshold caveat (partial-month re-run edge case). No blockers.

Non-blocking known gaps: compare table em-dashes (pre-existing market-stats data gap, not introduced by this run); DLD raw key names in JS bundle (not user-visible).
